data.narrative:
Kreios Space is a seed-stage company in Space & Aerospace that has raised €8M to date. The valuation landscape includes 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, anchored by SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Standard triangulation methods encounter material constraints. Peer-median pricing is not applicable: while 11 same-sector peers exist, none meet narrow filters for stage, adjacent stage, or similar-tier valuation clustering—a minimum of 3 datapoints is required for this method. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples is similarly inapplicable; the Space & Aerospace sector median P/S is 25.8x across the 6 public tickers, but Kreios' revenue is not on file, as is typical for private companies at seed stage. Without revenue disclosure, revenue-multiple methods cannot be deployed.

The absence of applicable quantitative methods reflects genuine scarcity constraints in the seed-stage space tech market rather than analytical failure. A defensible valuation would require either (1) bottom-up discounted cash flow modeling anchored to technical milestones and addressable market, (2) primary diligence on comparable seed rounds in adjacent propulsion, satellite, or launch subsectors, or (3) disclosed revenue or forward guidance from Kreios itself. The current data set does not support a point estimate or range with analytic rigor.
